key_risks: Semiconductor and Nasdaq high-beta positions could reverse sharply if Thursday's jobs report or ISM data disappoints
Sticky core PCE at 3.4% could push yields higher and pressure growth/tech multiples
Holiday-shortened week with thin liquidity may amplify volatility around economic releases
Crowded semiconductor trade after huge YTD gains carries elevated reversal/positioning risk

portfolio.rationale: Strong semiconductor momentum with broad chip strength on June 30 (NVDA, AMD, Intel up); leadership group heading into a light-catalyst holiday week. Momentum supported by sector-wide breadth, though very high beta and reversal risk noted.
Cyclical strength with strong 30d relative outperformance and high up-day share; benefits from constructive ISM Manufacturing readings and firm domestic demand data.
Defensive-leaning sector with strong recent breadth and low drawdown; biotech strength spillover provides fundamental support ahead of jobs data.
Broad large-cap tech/growth exposure participating in the Nasdaq's 1.5% June 30 rally; diversified way to hold leadership with lower single-sector concentration.
Broad participation with reduced mega-cap concentration; steady positive breadth and low volatility provide ballast against a tech reversal.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward leadership groups (semis, industrials, broad tech) with strong breadth and recent macro data supportive of soft-landing risk appetite, balanced by equal-weight and defensive healthcare for ballast in a holiday-shortened week.
rationale_summary: Overweight momentum-leading cyclicals and tech with diversified broad-market ballast to capture continued risk-on breadth while limiting concentration risk.
